title: Neurocysticercosis
chief_complaint: I had a seizure yesterday - my first ever. I'm from rural Mexico and moved here 2 years ago. I've had headaches for the past few months. I don't remember the seizure - my husband said I was shaking all over for 2 minutes.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Post-ictal confusion resolving. No focal deficits. Fundoscopy: No papilledema.

hidden_data.ct_head: Multiple cystic lesions with central hyperdense dots (scolex) - 'hole-with-dot' sign. Lesions in different stages: Some cystic, some ring-enhancing, some calcified. One lesion in right frontal lobe with surrounding edema.
hidden_data.mri_brain: Right frontal lobe: Vesicular stage cyst with scolex. Surrounding vasogenic edema. Left temporal: Calcified granuloma (chronic). Right parietal: Enhancing nodule (granular stage).
hidden_data.stool: Taenia solium ova present (patient also has intestinal taeniasis).

correct_diagnosis: Neurocysticercosis (Parenchymal, Multiple Lesions in Different Stages) with Secondary Seizure
key_learning_points: Neurocysticercosis = CNS infection by Taenia solium (pork tapeworm) larvae. Most common cause of acquired epilepsy worldwide
Humans are accidental intermediate hosts (normally pigs). Ingest eggs from contaminated food/water (fecal-oral). Also autoinfection if intestinal tapeworm present

Pathognomonic: Cystic lesion with scolex ('hole-with-dot' sign). Different stages: Vesicular (viable, no inflammation), Colloidal (dying, ring enhancement + edema), Granular (enhancing nodule), Calcified (inactive)
Treatment: Treat seizures with AEDs (Levetiracetam). Antiparasitic: Albendazole 15mg/kg/day + Praziquantel 50mg/kg/day ×10-14 days + Prednisone to reduce cerebral edema from dying larvae

CONTROVERSY: Treatment indicated for viable cysts. DO NOT treat calcified lesions (dead - no benefit). Caution: Multiple viable cysts → massive release of antigens can cause cerebral edema and death. Always give steroids with antiparasitics
